highlights:
Data Scientist I – Career Advising & Professional Development | MIT

# Data Scientist I
...
The Data Scientist I – Reliability and Maintenance Planning supports reliability, maintenance planning, and operational decision-making across Fleet and Equipment at TTX.
...
This role focuses on developing statistical and machine learning models to improve component reliability, optimize maintenance strategies, and enhance fleet performance. The position works with complex operational datasets (e.g., detector, movement, and billing data) and partners closely with Fleet, Engineering, Supply Chain, and other business stakeholders.
...
While the primary focus is reliability analytics, the role will also gain exposure to modern analytics platforms including Microsoft Fabric, Power Platform, and other AI-enabled solutions as part of TTX’s evolving data ecosystem.
...
This role is ideal for candidates interested in applying data science to real-
...
Reliability Modeling and Predictive Analytics (Primary)
...
- Develop and evaluate component failure and reliability models using statistical and machine learning techniques
...
Apply and benchmark methods such as Weibull analysis, survival modeling, and predictive ML approaches
...
Analyze operational datasets (detector, billing, movement/location) to identify failure patterns and risk drivers
...
Support maintenance strategy optimization and predictive maintenance initiatives
...
- Partner with Fleet, Engineering, and Supply Chain stakeholders to translate
...
problems into analytical solutions
...
- Support benchmarking and evaluation of
...
performance and maintenance strategies
...
- Communicate insights clearly through dashboards, presentations, and documentation
...
- Gain strong working knowledge
...
rail industry and associated data including billing, detector, movement/location, etc.
...
Data Analysis and Visualization
...
- Perform exploratory data analysis and feature engineering on complex, real-world operational datasets
- Clean and reconcile data across multiple systems and sources
- Develop reproducible datasets and modeling workflows
- Build Power BI dashboards and semantic models to deliver insights and support decision-making
- Assist in development of corporate metrics and program performance reporting
...
Contribute to automation of analytical workflows using Power Automate, PowerApps, or Python
...
Gain exposure to Microsoft Fabric for data workflows and model integration
...
Support development of AI-enabled solutions (e.g., Copilot Studio, Fabric Data Agents) as part of team initiatives
...
2+ years of professional experience in:
...
- Data science, analytics, OR reliability/predictive analytics
...
Experience with:
...
- Python (pandas, scikit-learn or equivalent)
- SQL for data extraction and transformation
- PowerBI or other visualization tool
- Messy, real-world operational data
- Analysis of large, multi-source datasets
...
Experience with:
...
- Reliability modeling (Weibull, survival analysis, failure modeling)
- Time series forecasting or predictive maintenance
- Statistical modeling and hypothesis testing
- Industrial, transportation, or asset-heavy data environment
- PySpark and AutoML
- Git/AzureDevOps and version control
- Basic understanding of data engineering concepts like ETL pipelines and CICD

results.title: Jacob Lynn
highlights:
Senior Data Analytics & Governance Manager at Textron Aviation
...
Senior Manager, Data Analytics & Automation at [Textron Aviation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/textron-aviation)
...
Manager leading IT team responsible for the design and support of data analytics infrastructure for Textron Aviation. This role builds on my experiences standing up an analytics team to serve as a champion for data and analytics across the entire organization. I believe strongly in building effective teams and I am a passionate advocate for the importance of data at the core of business strategy. Aviation has long been an inspiration for me. It shows us that things we once thought were impossible can become almost mundane and allows individuals all across the globe to become connected in a very tangible way that few other technologies provide. My passion for aviation, born from airline travel, has extended into a love of cars and driven me to become interested in pursuing a broad range of careers focused in transportation both on the ground and in the air. This passion translates into an energetic and invested attitude in the work environment, even when the tasks at hand deviate from an aviation focus. Specialties: Strong verbal and written communication and presentation skills, gained primarily through practice within Toastmasters International. Highly proficient in Alteryx and PowerBi, with skills specifically in data analysis and visualization. Excellent interpersonal skills with an aptitude for leadership. Keen eye for improving process efficiency.
...

Develop and lead execution of strategies for data analytics success across the business
...

Leading central data & analytics team for Textron Aviation, including managing support of data analytics infrastructure throughout the business. Primary responsibility is laying out a vision for a data driven culture and championing that growth across the entire organization.
...
#### Analytics Manager
...
Created and managed team of 6 data analysts supporting the Textron Aviation Global Parts & Distribution business. Led the creation and maintenance of a robust PowerBi ecosystem consisting of 75 reports measuring sales and operational metrics. Spearheaded development of advanced analytics such as two data driven pricing models and other predictive analytics. Awarded an internal innovation award for work on the data driven pricing model.

results.title: Software Engineer AI/ML – Office of Career Strategy – Yale University
highlights:
Software Engineer AI/ML – Office of Career Strategy – Yale University
...
# Software Engineer AI/ML
...
The CES Business Intelligence team is building the next generation of AI-powered solutions for commercial, contracts, and operations. We’re looking for an AI Engineer to help transform GE Aerospace operational data into production-grade machine learning pipelines, models, and LLM-powered applications.
...
This is a multi-faceted engineering role. You’ll spend most of your time developing AI/ML products by training models, developing applications, and creating APIs. You will partner closely with analytics teams to enable AI within our existing operational tools. You’ll also contribute to AI strategy and partner with executive stakeholders to align on requirements, success metrics, and business impact. We’re looking for someone who’s excited to expand their technical skillset in AI/ML and deliver advanced solutions that directly impact daily operations.
...
What you’ll do: Design, build, deliver, and maintain AI/ML products including LLM-powered applications, forecasting models, anomaly detection systems, and intelligent agents. Own the full AI/ML lifecycle: requirements analysis, model design, training, evaluation, API development, deployment, and operational support. Convert complex operational datasets into scalable AI capabilities that enable real-time decision support.
...

...
- Package AI/ML models as robust, well-documented APIs that enable seamless integration into dashboards, applications, and operational workflows.
...
- Collaborate with BI team to embed AI features into existing applications that enable natural language queries, predictive insights, and intelligent recommendations directly within user-facing applications

...
- We require a bachelors degree in a relevant discipline such as computer science, electrical engineering, mathematics, or a related engineering field. - We are looking for at least 5 years of experience in artificial intelligence or a closely related area. - We need a strong foundation in software engineering, AI, and machine learning. - We value solid expertise in signal processing theory and practical applications, including radar, communications, electronic warfare, sonar, audio, or biomedical domains. - We expect experience with modern AI methods such as computer vision, deep learning, and reinforcement learning. - We prefer proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Java, or C++. - We prefer hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX, or Keras. - We prefer experience using Python libraries such as NumPy, OpenCV, scikit-learn, pandas, and SciPy. - We look for self-motivation, a strong work ethic, and effective time management. - We value excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
...
## Responsibilities
...
- We develop and apply AI technologies to address complex signal processing challenges and deliver innovative solutions. - We contribute across the full system development lifecycle, including requirements definition, system and software design, implementation, integration, and flight testing. - We take ownership of critical assignments and lead technical teams to produce high-quality outcomes. - We help craft proposals and white papers to support funding for AI research and development efforts. - We serve in technical lead roles and mentor junior engineers to strengthen their capabilities. - We collaborate with cross-functional partners to build and deploy AI solutions. - We explain complex technical ideas and solution concepts to non-technical stakeholders. - We work with other teams across our organization to identify and advance opportunities for AI adoption.

We offer the opportunity to work on high-impact programs ranging from early-stage research to deployment in a fast-paced, innovative, and collaborative environment. We also support professional growth through mentorship and development opportunities. Preferred work locations are our facilities in Fort Worth, Texas, or Palmdale, California, and remote arrangements may be considered for qualified candidates.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
